overview_paragraph: Berlin shines from late spring through early fall (May–September), when lakeside beaches open, biergartens spill onto sidewalks, and the days stretch past 9pm. June and July bring open-air cinema, club season, and a wave of festivals; August stays warm but a touch quieter. Fall (September–October) is crisp and cultural—marathon weekend, Festival of Lights, golden Tiergarten—while deep winter (November–February) turns grey and cold, the cue for museums, Berlinale in February, and glühwein at Christmas markets. March and April wake the city up with the first sun on Tempelhofer Feld.
January: Grey-sky museum days on Museum Island, glühwein-warm cafés, and post-holiday sales along Kurfürstendamm.
Cold, quiet streets, steamy Kaffee und Kuchen stops, and the calm before Berlinale buzz returns.
February: Berlinale red carpets at Potsdamer Platz, packed arthouse screenings, and film talk in smoky Kreuzberg bars.
Winter light over the Spree, long gallery afternoons, and festival energy that thaws the coldest month.
March: First sun on Tempelhofer Feld, MaerzMusik's avant-garde nights, and cafés easing back onto the pavement.
Spree-side walks shedding their chill, daffodils in Tiergarten, and the city stretching toward spring.
April: Cherry blossoms in the Gardens of the World, kite-flying on the old runways, and biergartens reopening.
Mild blue-sky wanders through Prenzlauer Berg, Easter-market stalls, and longer, brighter evenings.
May: Karneval der Kulturen drumming through Kreuzberg, Gallery Weekend openings, and 1. Mai street parties.
Lilac-scented courtyards, lakeside days warming up, and Cup-final roar from the Olympiastadion.
June: Christopher Street Day energy building, Fête de la Musique on every corner, and lakes opening at Wannsee.
Golden 9pm light, packed beer gardens, and warm nights when the club queues stretch down the block.
July: Hot afternoons at Müggelsee, open-air cinema under the stars, and rooftop bars buzzing till dawn.
Spree swims at Badeschiff, Späti beers on the canal, and a city living fully outdoors.
August: Lazy lake days, the Lange Nacht der Museen, and warm evenings drifting between summer street festivals.
Quieter boulevards, shaded biergartens, and balmy nights when Berlin feels easy and unhurried.
September: Marathon roar through the Brandenburg Gate, Pyronale fireworks over the Olympiastadion, and mild golden days.
Crisp-edged sunshine, harvest menus in Neukölln, and the last warm lake afternoons of the year.
October: Festival of Lights painting the landmarks aglow, Unity Day crowds, and amber leaves across Tiergarten.
Cozy autumn cafés, illuminated facades after dark, and the season turning toward indoor culture.
November: Grey, contemplative days for Museum Island, candlelit wine bars, and St. Martin lanterns through the gloaming.
Foggy Spree mornings, gallery afternoons, and the first Christmas-market scaffolding going up.
December: Glühwein at Gendarmenmarkt, twinkling stalls at Charlottenburg Palace, and Silvester fireworks at the gate.
RAW and Alexanderplatz markets aglow, roasted-almond air, and the city counting down to a roaring New Year.
